name: Cabeza Modorra y embalse La Toba – circular desde Beamud
region_intro: Mountain biking around Beamud offers a diverse landscape characterized by the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, featuring river sources, hills, and reservoirs. The region's terrain includes varied elevations suitable for different skill levels, from moderate climbs to more challenging ascents. Mountain bike trails in Beamud navigate through natural areas, providing a mix of forest paths and open sections. Best mountain bike trails around Beamud The most popular mountain bike trail is Nacimiento del río Tajo – circular desde Valdemeca por el Parque Natural Serranía de Cuenca, a 30.5 miles (49.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the source of the Tagus River within a natural park setting. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cabeza Modorra y embalse La Toba – circular desde Beamud, a moderate 24.3 miles (39.1 km) path. This circular route from Beamud features views of Cabeza Modorra hill and La Toba reservoir. Local mountain bikers also love the Refugio de la Alconera – Refugio La Alconera loop from Huélamo, a 29.0 miles (46.6 km) trail leading through forested areas and past a mountain refuge, often completed in about 4 hours 1 minute. Mountain biking around Beamud is defined by natural park landscapes, river valleys, and reservoir views. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Beamud are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Beamud's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain bike trails can I expect around Beamud?
A: Mountain biking around Beamud offers a diverse landscape within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. You'll find trails navigating through river sources, hills, and reservoirs, with a mix of forest paths and open sections. The terrain includes varied elevations, suitable for riders looking for moderate to challenging ascents.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Beamud?
A: The trails around Beamud are generally categorized as moderate to difficult. While there are no routes specifically listed as 'easy', those with some mountain biking experience might find the moderate trails manageable. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016865909/cabeza-modorra-y-embalse-la-toba-circular-desde-beamud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza Modorra y embalse La Toba – circular desde Beamud&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of mountain biking in Beamud?
A: The mountain bike trails in Beamud are primarily suited for riders with some experience, offering a mix of moderate and difficult routes. There are 3 moderate and 3 difficult trails available, providing options for those seeking a good workout and technical challenges.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting directly from Beamud?
A: Yes, a notable circular route starting from Beamud is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016865909/cabeza-modorra-y-embalse-la-toba-circular-desde-beamud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza Modorra y embalse La Toba – circular desde Beamud&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 24.3-mile (39.1 km) path offers views of Cabeza Modorra hill and La Toba reservoir.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge can explore routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016891197/nacimiento-del-rio-tajo-circular-desde-valdemeca-por-el-parque-natural-serrania-de-cuenca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del río Tajo – circular desde Valdemeca por el Parque Natural Serranía de Cuenca&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 30.5-mile (49.2 km) trail explores the source of the Tagus River within the natural park. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38141718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huélamo – Spring and washhouse loop from Molatilla del Molino&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 60 miles (97.5 km) with significant elevation gain.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Beamud?
A: Many trails offer scenic views and natural attractions. You can find several viewpoints, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3790692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de la Tola Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5631448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint over the La Toba Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. There are also natural monuments like a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5526726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Water Spring&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5403954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boquerón Cave&lt;/a&gt; to explore.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that pass by reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region is known for its reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016865909/cabeza-modorra-y-embalse-la-toba-circular-desde-beamud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza Modorra y embalse La Toba – circular desde Beamud&lt;/a&gt; route specifically features views of the La Toba reservoir. You can also find viewpoints overlooking the reservoir, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5631448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint over the La Toba Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Beamud?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Riders often praise the natural park landscapes, river valleys, and reservoir views, highlighting the diverse terrain and the mix of forest paths and open sections that make for an engaging ride.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Beamud?
A: Given its location in the Serranía de Cuenca, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Beamud, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be hot, especially at midday, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the source of the Tagus River?
A: Yes, the challenging route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016891197/nacimiento-del-rio-tajo-circular-desde-valdemeca-por-el-parque-natural-serrania-de-cuenca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del río Tajo – circular desde Valdemeca por el Parque Natural Serranía de Cuenca&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes you to the source of the Tagus River within the natural park.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that go through forested areas?
A: Many of the trails in Beamud navigate through natural areas with significant forest cover. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38142525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio de la Alconera – Refugio La Alconera loop from Huélamo&lt;/a&gt; leads through extensive forested areas and past a mountain refuge.
tips: The La Toba reservoir was built on the course of the Júcar river, taking advantage of its gorges as it passed through the Ceja de Peña Betaya and Monteagudillo. It is surrounded by a picturesque and beautiful landscape that is worth visiting.
The top of Monteagudillo, at just over 1,340 meters above sea level, offers wonderful views in all directions, highlighting the beautiful landscape that surrounds the La Toba reservoir.
